steps.title: Identify the claim to illustrate
steps.content: The passage states the speaker claims to feel protected by the mountain. Any supporting quotation must show the speaker experiencing shelter or protection from the mountain — not its appearance, location, or the speaker's movement.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — "A dry ravine emerged under boughs / Into the pasture.": Describes a landscape feature (a ravine under trees). No sense of shelter or protection for the speaker. ✗
  • Choice B — "The mountain stood there to be pointed at.": Describes the mountain's passive, visible presence. Violates relevance — being “pointed at” conveys landmark status, not protection. ✗
  • Choice C — "I felt it like a wall / Behind which I was sheltered from a wind.": The speaker explicitly says “I felt it” and “sheltered from a wind” — a direct match for feeling protected. ✓
  • Choice D — "I crossed the river and swung round the mountain.": Describes the speaker's physical movement around the mountain. No protection or shelter expressed. ✗
That is the correct answer.
